html:
GAF's Commercial Division on Laredo Roofs

GAF is best known nationally as the largest shingle manufacturer for residential roofing, but the company also runs a substantial commercial roofing division with its own single-ply, built-up, and coating product lines. We spec GAF commercial products on a portion of our Laredo jobs, particularly where their specific TPO or coating line fits the project better than an alternative manufacturer's.

A Residential Giant's Commercial Side

GAF is headquartered in Parsippany, New Jersey, and its name recognition comes overwhelmingly from residential asphalt shingles. The commercial division operates as a separate product and specification track aimed at low-slope roofing - TPO, modified bitumen, and built-up systems - rather than the pitched-roof shingle products the brand is best known for. It's worth understanding that distinction, since a manufacturer's residential reputation doesn't automatically say much about their commercial single-ply product quality, and we evaluate the two sides of the business independently when we're deciding whether to spec their products on a given job.

EverGuard and the Rest of the Commercial Line

GAF's commercial TPO product line, marketed under names like EverGuard, covers standard thickness options similar to what other major manufacturers offer, along with modified bitumen and built-up roofing products for buildings where those systems fit better than single-ply membrane. Their catalog also includes cover boards, insulation, and fastening accessories meant to be specified together as a complete assembly rather than sourced piecemeal from unrelated suppliers.

Built-Up and Modified Bitumen Products

Beyond single-ply TPO, GAF's commercial line includes built-up roofing and modified bitumen products that see use on some of the older downtown Laredo buildings where those systems are the better structural match, similar to the modified bitumen work described on our SBS roof-systems page. Having a manufacturer with both a modern single-ply catalog and an established built-up and modified bitumen line lets us keep a consistent manufacturer relationship on a mixed portfolio of properties rather than juggling separate suppliers for every roof type an owner manages.

Coatings and Recover Products

Beyond new membrane systems, GAF's commercial line includes reflective coating products used in recoat and recover projects, similar to the coating work described on our silicone roof coating page. We evaluate GAF's coating products against other manufacturers' options the same way we evaluate their membrane products - on fit for the specific roof, not brand preference.

  • Product line reviewed against the building's specific traffic and exposure profile
  • Membrane thickness selected to match anticipated roof use, not a default option
  • Coating or recover products evaluated against alternatives from other manufacturers
  • Component compatibility confirmed before mixing accessories across product lines
  • Warranty documentation filed and explained to the owner in plain terms

Where GAF Commercial Fits Alongside Other Manufacturers

We don't treat GAF as a default or a fallback; their commercial products get specified when they're the right fit for a particular roof, the same as Carlisle, Versico, Johns Manville, or any other manufacturer we work with. Product availability, specific technical requirements, and sometimes owner preference from a prior project all factor into which manufacturer we recommend for a given job.

Because GAF's national footprint depends primarily on its residential shingle distribution network, commercial product availability in a market the size of Laredo can vary more by season and by specific product than it does for a manufacturer whose entire business centers on commercial single-ply. We confirm current lead times on the specific GAF commercial product before committing to it on a project with a firm schedule, rather than assuming national scale automatically means fast local availability.

Every manufacturer covered on our manufacturers pages competes in roughly the same commercial single-ply and modified bitumen categories, and GAF's specific advantage on a given bid usually comes down to a particular product's price at the time, a distributor's current stock in this region, or occasionally an owner's existing relationship with the brand from a prior project elsewhere in their portfolio. We present GAF alongside Carlisle, Versico, Johns Manville, Sika Sarnafil, and Elevate in every proposal where more than one manufacturer's product would reasonably work, rather than steering an owner toward one name without showing the full comparison in writing.

Reading a GAF Commercial Warranty

Like other manufacturers, GAF's commercial warranty terms vary by product line and by how a project is documented and installed. We don't summarize warranty terms generically in a proposal - we pull the actual document for the specific product being installed and go through it with the owner, since exclusions and maintenance obligations differ enough between product lines that a generic summary would be misleading.

Questions About GAF Commercial Roofing

Is GAF's commercial roofing the same quality as their residential shingles?

They're different product lines built to different specifications for different roof types; we evaluate GAF's commercial products on their own merits rather than assuming residential reputation carries over.

What's EverGuard?

It's the brand name under which GAF markets part of its commercial TPO membrane line.

Do you recommend GAF over other manufacturers?

We recommend whichever manufacturer's product fits the specific building and overall budget best; GAF is one of several manufacturers we regularly work with, not a default choice.

Does GAF offer recover and coating products for existing roofs?

Yes, their commercial line includes reflective coating and recover products alongside their new membrane systems.

Where is GAF headquartered?

Parsippany, New Jersey, though the commercial roofing division's manufacturing and distribution operations are spread across multiple facilities nationally.
